messages.content: Do oceny intensywności tworzenia biofilmu wykorzystano gęstość optyczną odcięcia (ODc) i biofilm definiowano jako nieprzylegające/brak biofilmu (-) OD600 < Odc; słaby (+) ODc < OD600 < 2 x ODc; umiarkowany (++) 2 x ODc < OD600 < 4 x ODc; silny (+++) 4 x ODc < OD600. - rephrase this

Biofilm formation intensity was assessed using the cutoff optical density (ODc), where the presence or absence of biofilm was defined as follows: no biofilm/absence (-) when OD600 < ODc, weak (+) when ODc < OD600 < 2 x ODc, moderate (++) when 2 x ODc < OD600 < 4 x ODc, and strong (+++) when OD600 > 4 x ODc.

Based on the experimental data, models of the response surface of two independent variables (time and inoculum concentration) were developed. Table 6 presents the estimations of function parameters of the developed models, which have 4-6 parameters. The most developed (n=5) are four-parameter models. - rephrase this

The response surface models for the two independent variables (time and inoculum concentration) were developed based on the experimental data. Table 6 provides the estimated parameters for the developed models, which range from 4 to 6 parameters. Among the models, the most advanced ones are the four-parameter models, with a total of five models in this category.

The coefficient of determination/regression R 2 is used as a general measure of the fit of the estimated model to the empirical data and the independent variables' explanation of the variability of the dependent variable. The coefficient of determination is a measure of the strength of the linear relationship between the data. - rephrase this

The coefficient of determination, also known as R-squared, is commonly used as a measure to assess the goodness of fit of an estimated model to the observed data and to determine how well the independent variables explain the variability in the dependent variable. It quantifies the strength of the linear relationship between the variables in question.
Bacillus spp., C. sakazakii, and E. cloacae are known as biofilm-forming microorganisms (Gemba et al., 2022) and are often isolated from food-contact surfaces. - rephrase this

Gemba et al. (2022) have identified Bacillus spp., C. sakazakii, and E. cloacae as microorganisms that are capable of forming biofilms. These organisms are commonly found on surfaces in contact with food.
Among the tested bacteria, E. cloacae colonized the surface of enteral tubes the fastest (after 6 hours). - rephrase this
Out of the bacteria tested, E. cloacae demonstrated the most rapid colonization of the enteral tube surfaces, achieving colonization after a duration of 6 hours.
The 4 °C temperature was chosen because of its importance in the food cold chain. - rephrase this
The reason for selecting a temperature of 4 °C was its significance within the context of the food cold chain.
In our study, all strains showed no growth, at refrigeration temperature, but produced a biofilm from 36 hours on both tested surfaces. - rephrase this

In our study, it was observed that all strains did not exhibit any growth at refrigeration temperature. However, a biofilm was produced by all strains starting from 36 hours on both surfaces that were tested.

Optimum temperature conditions for biofilm development cannot be unequivocally determined. For some strains, lowering the incubation temperature may reduce bacterial growth but promote biofilm formation. This phenomenon may constitute a significant health risk. Storing milk or milk replacer in a bottle at refrigerated temperature may contribute to the formation of a biofilm on the surface of the material. - rephrase this

Determining the exact optimal temperature conditions for biofilm development is challenging due to variability among strains. Interestingly, in certain strains, decreasing the incubation temperature can inhibit bacterial growth while promoting biofilm formation. This presents a potential health risk. Storing milk or milk replacer in a refrigerated bottle may facilitate the formation of a biofilm on the material's surface.
The models developed mostly confirmed the influence of the factors considered in the experiment as determining biofilm formation. - rephrase this
The developed models largely validated the significance of the factors examined in the experiment in determining biofilm formation.

Inadequate, too low concentration of e.g. ethanol as a disinfectant may cause environmental stress of microorganisms, thus increasing the potential risk of biofilm formation (Tango et al., 2018). Due to reports of the risk of breast milk contamination and the occurrence of C. sakazakii, B. cereus and E. cloacae infections in newborns and the ability of these bacteria to produce biofilm (Haston et al., 2023; Lachowska et al., 2021; Akineden et al. ., 2017; Block et al., 2002; van Acker et al., 2001; McMullan et al., 2018; Zeng et al. 2018; Chaves et al., 2018; Bowen et al., 2017; Decousser et al., 2013; Pestourie et al., 2014; Weems et al., 2015; Sundararajan et al., 2018; Jackson et al., 2015, Gemba et al., 2022), the models obtained as a result of research can be a tool in the assessment of microbiological risk in Breast Milk Banks, neonatal wards, neonatal intensive care units - rephrase this

Insufficient or excessively low concentrations of disinfectants like ethanol can impose environmental stress on microorganisms, thereby increasing the potential risk of biofilm formation (Tango et al., 2018). Considering the reported risk of breast milk contamination and the occurrence of infections in newborns caused by C. sakazakii, B. cereus, and E. cloacae, all of which have the ability to produce biofilms (Haston et al., 2023; Lachowska et al., 2021; Akineden et al., 2017; Block et al., 2002; van Acker et al., 2001; McMullan et al., 2018; Zeng et al. 2018; Chaves et al., 2018; Bowen et al., 2017; Decousser et al., 2013; Pestourie et al., 2014; Weems et al., 2015; Sundararajan et al., 2018; Jackson et al., 2015; Gemba et al., 2022), the models derived from this research can serve as a valuable tool for assessing the microbiological risks in Breast Milk Banks, neonatal wards, and neonatal intensive care units.

Analysis of biofilm formation on the polypropylene surface at 4 °C, for all bacteria analyzed, indicates 24 hours as the maximum time for safe storage in bottled milk. The models developed in this case obtained satisfactory assessments of parameters and fit to the data. The risk of biofilm formation depends on the density of the microbial inoculum. - rephrase this

The investigation of biofilm formation on polypropylene surfaces at 4 °C across all analyzed bacteria suggests that a maximum safe storage time for bottled milk is within 24 hours. The models developed in this study demonstrated satisfactory evaluations of parameters and exhibited a good fit to the data. It should be noted that the risk of biofilm formation is contingent upon the density of the microbial inoculum.
